violations.text: Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener, slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC moved can opener to 3-bay sink to be washed, rinsed and sanitized. Slicer shall be dissembled and washed, rinsed and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Observed Quinoa in covered plastic containers cooling in walk-in cooler at 83 degrees Fahrenheit. Quinoa was uncovered and proper cooling methods reviewed with PIC. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2025
Cleaned and sanitized equipment and utensils are not stored inverted. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2025
Dishes/equipment was observed stored on top of each other wet before properly air drying. PIC unstacked dishes/equipment. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2025

The cutting boards in the prep area are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. PIC elected disposal of worn cutting board. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine has an accumulation of biofilm. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2025

The ice machine is lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2025
The floors in the storage area has an accumulation of old mouse droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2025

Various food items were observed held at 47-50 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Items placed in the unit under 3 hours prior to visit were moved to walk-in refrigeration to rapidly cool. Items placed in the unit over 4 hours prior to visit were voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/12/2023

The flip top prep unit located next to the pancake/waffle station was observed maintaining an ambient temperature of 45 degrees Fahrenheit. Unit was serviced the week prior of visit as stated by PIC. Unit was taken out of service until it can be replaced/serviced/repaired. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/12/2023

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was 100 parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be 150-400 ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/28/2023

Yogurt, eggs, cheese and other items were observed held at 45-50 degrees Fahrenheit in a prep unit that is maintaining an ambient temperature of 50 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Person in charge moved items placed in the unit 2 hours prior to inspection to proper refrigeration. Cheese left in the unit overnight were voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/28/2023

Working containers of dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 07/12/2023

The flip top prep unit located next to the pancake/waffle station was observed maintaining an ambient temperature of 50 degrees Fahrenheit. Unit was taken out of service until it can be replaced/serviced/repaired. New Violation. Correct By: 07/12/2023
